content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 27, 1989

Daniel E. Stone
Rossmoor Community Services District
3232 Hedwig Road
Rossmoor, CA 90720

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-89-670

Dear Mr. Stone:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Rossmoor Community Services District Board Member
Gustave Brickman regarding the mass mailing provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your request is more
of a general inquiry than a request for advice as to a specific
action pending before the board, we treat your request as one for
informal assistance.²

QUESTION

Under what circumstances may board members suggest items to
be printed in the district's newsletter?

CONCLUSION

The mass mailing provisions of the Act do not prevent board
members from suggesting items to be printed in the district's
newsletter. If board members suggest items to be printed in the
newsletter, the newsletter will be deemed to be prepared in co-
operation, consultation, coordination or concert with the board

_____________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-89-640
Page 2                © 70

members. Therefore, it may not contain the name, office or other
reference to the board members or their photographs.

FACTS

You are the general manager of the Rossmoor Community
Services District. One of the board members, Gustave Brickman,
wants to know if board members can suggest items, at an open board
meeting or otherwise, to be printed in the district's newsletter.
The suggestions would relate to items about which the public
should be informed or items of interest to residents of the com-
munity. The items suggested would have nothing to do with
individual board members. For example, one item might concern the
problem of vehicles parking on the street on street sweeping days.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 of the Act provides that "no newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense." A mass mail-
ing is defined as two hundred or more substantially similar pieces
of mail. (Section 82041.5.) Regulation 18901 clarifies that the
purpose of Section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from using
public funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings at
public expense.

We have previously advised your district that a mass mailing
may not be sent at public expense by a community services district
if it contains the name, office, photograph or any other reference
to an elected officer of the district and is prepared or sent in
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the
elected officer. (Regulation 18901(c)(2); Aleshire Advice Letter,
No. I-89-539, copies enclosed.)³

We believe that suggestions from board members as to the
contents of the district's newsletter result in the newsletter
being prepared in cooperation, consultation, coordination or
concert with the board members. Therefore, the newsletter may not
contain the name, office, photograph or any other reference to the
board members, except as otherwise permitted by the regulation.
For example, the names of the board members may be listed in a
roster listing of the district officials in which all are listed
in the same typeface, type size and location in the layout of the
newsletter. (Regulation 18901(e); Krannawitter Advice Letter,
No. A-89-300, copy enclosed.)⁴

_________________________

³ At its meeting of December 13, 1989, the Commission adopted a
new version of Regulation 18901. A copy of the revised regulation
is enclosed for your review. We are not yet advising pursuant to
the new regulation. In this instance, our advice would not change
under the revised version of the regulation.

⁴ The revised version of Regulation 18901 also requires that the
listing be in the same type color.

Our File No. I-89-640
Page 3                                ©7C


     It should be noted that this advice does not prevent members
from making suggestions for the newsletter or prevent the newslet-
ter from being prepared and sent at public expense. It merely
limits references to the elected officer.

     I trust that this letter clarifies the application of the
mass mailing provisions of the Act with respect to your question.
If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 322-
5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]

                                        By:  Margaret W. Ellison
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED/MWE/aa

Enclosures

November 15, 1989


Gregory W. Baugher
Executive Director
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Rossmoor Community Services District Newsletter.

Dear Mr. Baugher,

Rossmoor Community Services District Board Member Gustave Brickman wants to know if board members can suggest items, at an open Board Meeting or other-wise, to be printed in the District's Newsletter if the items are things that the District should be informing the public about or if the items are of interest to residents of the community and have nothing to do with in-dividual board members. For instance, printing an item about a problem in the District of vehicles parking on the street on the District's street sweeping days.

I look forward to your reply.

Sincerely,

Daniel E. Stone
General Manager

cc: Rossmoor Community Services District Board

November 20, 1989


Daniel E. Stone
General Manager, Rossmoor Community
Services District
3232 Hedwig Road
Rossmoor, CA  90720

Re:  Letter No. 89-670

Dear Mr. Stone:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on November 20, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
